title: Invalid Heap Operations Result in Incorrect Max Heap

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return true if the input list is a valid max heap and false otherwise.
statement.notes: The input list may contain duplicate elements. Time complexity should be O(n) and space complexity should be O(1).

examples.explanation: The input is a valid max heap.
The input is not a valid max heap.

hints.text: First hint text: Check if the input list satisfies the heap property.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { int n = nums.size(); for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { int val = nums[i]; int parent = (i-1)/2; if (parent >= 0) { if (nums[parent] >= val) { continue; } else { nums[parent] = val; } } } return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums) { int n = nums.length; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { int val = nums[i]; int parent = (i-1)/2; if (parent >= 0) { if (nums[parent] >= val) { continue; } else { nums[parent] = val; } } } return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: n = len(nums); for i in range(n): val = nums[i]; parent = (i-1)//2; if parent >= 0: if nums[parent] >= val: continue else: nums[parent] = val; return True;
